title: OnePlus Ace 7T specs leak, gigantic battery included
description: Specs for the upcoming OnePlus Ace 7 were leaked last month, and today the OnePlus Ace 7T is getting the exact same treatment. As the name implies, this should be the successor to the Ace 6T, which was launched last December. OnePlus is allegedly already working on the Ace 7T, which will have a screen with "1.5K" resolution and 185Hz refresh rate, as well as a 10,000 mAh battery. That may in fact be the biggest battery capacity ever seen in a OnePlus phone. OnePlus Ace 6T The Ace 7T will reportedly be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Gen 6 SoC, keep in mind that we're talking about the...

title: Valve: Steam Machine and Steam Frame are coming this summer
description: In a short blog, Valve confirmed that the Steam Machine and Steam Frame devices, which were announced in November last year, are coming this summer. However, the blog post was about the Verified program. The blog post itself is aimed at developers and announces that the Steam Machine and Steam Frame are being included in the Verified program. The said program launched with the Steam Deck and allows users to know how the game runs on the handheld before buying. According to Valve, the Steam Machine's requirements to get a Verified badge will be almost identical to the Steam Deck. As...

title: Oppo Pad 5 Matte Display (aka Air5) battery life and charging test
description: We're done with the battery life and charging tests of the Oppo Pad 5 Matte Display (also known as the Pad Air5). The tablet combines a 10,050mAh battery with the 4nm Dimensity 7300 Ultra and a 12.1-inch 120Hz IPS LCD, which is a notable selling point on this machine. It's the Matte Display Edition, essentially an anti-glare screen. It's TÜV Rheinland certified for a 97% cut to reflections and a 70% reduction in harmful blue light. It delivers in real life, too. It's easily legible in strong daylight, making it a good companion for some on-the-go web work. Alright, on to...

title: vivo X Fold6 to come with a 6,900mAh battery
description: Rumors surrounding vivo's upcoming foldable, the X Fold6, have pointed to a large 7,000mAh battery. Now, the device has reportedly surfaced on the 3C certification database, seemingly corroborating those leaks. According to tipster Experience More, who spotted the vivo X Fold6 on China's 3C certification platform, the foldable will feature a dual-cell battery with a typical capacity of 6,900mAh. Screenshots shared by the tipster reveal rated battery capacities of 2,807mAh and 3,863mAh for the two cells, adding up to a combined rated capacity of 6,670mAh. If the certification...

title: Ultrahuman informs users of breach, but passwords and payment info are safe
description: Ultrahuman founder and CEO, Mohit Kumar, informed users via email that there has been a security breach in Ultrahuman's system. The breach involves customer contact and account details, but no passwords, payment info, or credit card numbers. Ultrahuman says the affected info is contact and account details, order and transaction history, and some fitness-related data. On March 27, an unauthorized third party gained access to an internal system used by Ultrahuman for analytics. The company identified the incident and took the affected system down. Because of its design, the affected...

title: vivo V70 Lite is now official with Dimensity 7400 Turbo chip and 90W charging
description: Without much fanfare, vivo quietly introduced its V70 Lite midranger. The device is now available in the UAE, and it brings a familiar spec sheet and design to its predecessor, with one of the only changes being a higher-clocked chipset. The V70 Lite is equipped with MediaTek’s Dimensity 7400 Turbo chip, which replaces the V60 Lite’s Dimensity 7360 SoC. As their names imply, these chips are quite similar, with the only difference being the 7400’s ARM Cortex-A78 performance cores clocked at 2.6 GHz vs 2.5 GHz on the 7360. The new phone also features 8GB LPDDR4X RAM and 128/256GB UFS 3.1...

title: Xiaomi 17T series' launch in China next week marks the T lineup's debut in the country
description: Xiaomi unveiled the 17T and 17T Pro last week, and introduced the former in India yesterday. Now we learn that the two 17T series smartphones will launch in China on June 8, marking the debut of the T Series in Xiaomi's home country. The Xiaomi 17T and 17T Pro both run Android 16-based HyperOS 3 out of the box. However, the 17T is powered by the Dimensity 8500 Ultra SoC, while the 17T Pro has the Dimensity 9500 chip at the helm. The two smartphones also differ in their displays and batteries. The Xiaomi 17T comes with a 6.59" 120Hz 1,268p AMOLED display and a 6,500 mAh Si/C battery...

title: iPhone 18 Pro Max dimensions tipped
description: The iPhone 18 Pro series is expected to carry the same basic design as its predecessors and that will naturally extend to its dimensions. According to a new post from tipster Ice Universe, the 18 Pro Max (and we assume the 18 Pro) will measure 8.75 mm. That’s the same exact thickness as the 17 Pro models, which should not come as a surprise. The tipster further emphasizes that Apple’s continued focus on its first foldable phone – the iPhone Ultra has left the 18 Pro series without many novel features. This makes perfect sense as the Ultra or whatever it ends up being called will sit at...

title: Samsung starts testing One UI 9 for the Galaxy S25 series
description: Samsung has released two Android 17-based One UI 9 beta updates for the Galaxy S26 series, and it's even testing One UI 9 for the unannounced Galaxy S26 FE. Now we hear the brand has started internal testing for the Galaxy S25 series smartphones as well. We don't know the build version of One UI 9 that Samsung is testing, but we expect the Korean brand to release the first One UI 9 beta for the Galaxy S25 lineup in the coming weeks. Regarding the official debut of One UI 9, Samsung said that the upcoming folding smartphones will be the first to run it out of the box. Samsung is...

title: OnePlus Turbo 6X, Turbo 6X Pro design and key specs officially revealed
description: After confirming that the OnePlus Turbo 6X and OnePlus Turbo 6X Pro are set to launch in China soon, the company has now unveiled the phones' designs, color options, key specifications, and RAM/storage variants. Both devices have been listed on OnePlus' official online store in China. Additionally, OnePlus China President Louis Li Jie has revealed that the Turbo 6X series will debut next week, although the company has yet to announce an exact launch date. OnePlus Turbo 6X (left) and OnePlus Turbo 6X Pro (right) The OnePlus Turbo 6X is confirmed to be powered by the MediaTek...

title: Google brings Gemini to Android Go devices
description: Android (Go Edition) is made for low-end devices, optimized for low storage, low RAM, and low processing power. Now Google has announced that it's bringing Gemini to Android (Go Edition) phones, which have at least 2GB of RAM. It's called Gemini Go. 2GB has been the mandated minimum for (Go Edition) devices since Android 13 (Go Edition), so there are quite a few of them out there that meet this requirement. Gemini Go is described by Google as "a streamlined version of Gemini designed to help you stay connected and get things done, even on devices with lower storage". It replaces...

title: This is when the OnePlus 16 and iQOO 16 are rumored to be launching
description: Yesterday we heard that Xiaomi, Oppo, and vivo would all launch their upcoming flagship smartphone families in September. But what about sub-brands? Now the prolific Digital Chat Station on Weibo tells us what to expect from OnePlus and iQOO, specifically when the OnePlus 16 and iQOO 16 are going to launch. If you guessed September, you win. For reference, note that the OnePlus 15 and iQOO 15 both launched in October last year in China. OnePlus 15 This September is going to be a very full month for smartphone launches in China, it seems. Of course, many of these devices will also...

title: Xiaomi 18 Pro is getting a bigger rear screen
description: Back in September of last year, Xiaomi launched the 17 Pro and the 17 Pro Max which both feature a rear screen. The Xiaomi 17 Pro's rear display is a 2.7-inch panel. This, however, is now rumored to grow in its successor, the Xiaomi 18 Pro. According to a leaker on Weibo, this device's rear screen will measure a whopping 4 inches diagonally. Xiaomi 17 Pro Not just that, but it will be a higher-brightness panel than the Xiaomi 17 Pro's, and will be higher-resolution too, leading to a higher pixels-per-inch number. The rear screen will have large rounded corners, and there's also...

title: RedMagic Astra 2 gets certified ahead of release this month, here are the main specs
description: The next RedMagic tablet is launching in China as the RedMagic Tablet 5 Pro this month, that much has already been confirmed by the brand. We expect this to be branded Astra 2 internationally, since the original Astra was a rebranded Tablet 3 Pro for global markets, and the number 4 was skipped due to tetraphobia). Today the RedMagic Astra 2 has been certified for sale in China by the relevant authorities, and this process has revealed that it has support for 80W wired charging. The prolific Chinese leaker who goes by Digital Chat Station on Weibo says the Astra 2 will have a 9-inch...

title: Xiaomi 18 Pro Max camera details leak, two 200MP sensors are in
description: A new rumor from China today claims the upcoming Xiaomi 18 Pro Max will have a 200MP main camera using a 1/1.28" type "new generation" LOFIC sensor. The phone also allegedly has a 200MP periscope telephoto camera with 3x optical zoom and f/2.4 aperture. This camera uses a 1/1.56" type sensor. The telephoto camera will support macro shooting from approximately 15cm. This is a wildly different setup compared to the Xiaomi 17 Pro Max's 50MP main camera and 50MP periscope telephoto camera with 5x optical zoom. The telemacro focusing will be closer in the Xiaomi 18 Pro Max too, 15cm vs....

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8 to have both Exynos and Snapdragon chips
description: We've already heard that Samsung would be using the Exynos 2600 SoC in the upcoming Galaxy Z Flip8, but today a new report from a Korean tipster brings some additional context. Apparently, the company will use both the Exynos 2600 and a Qualcomm chip (presumably the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5), and which one you get will depend on your region. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8 leaked CAD-based render Samsung has done this before, it's nothing new, and if history repeats then expect North America to get the Snapdragon version for sure. What's interesting is that the source of this rumor claims...

title: Apple may have cancelled all Vision Pro successors, black version leaks
description: Apple may be working on a black Vision Pro headset. Images purportedly depicting it have been outed (see below), but it's unclear if this will actually be launched as an alternative to the white version that's been out for a while now, or if perhaps this was just a prototype that the company has scratched during the development process. Apple vison pro black color did you like? pic.twitter.com/J9JIYhbWmq— pipfix (@LusiRoy8) June 3, 2026 To add to the intrigue, Ming-Chi Kuo has now revised his roadmap for Apple's smart glasses and headsets, which he originally published last year. In...

title: Samsung introduces next-gen Galaxy Watch features with the new Samsung Health app update
description: Samsung has announced a major update to the Samsung Health app, which comes with a UI redesign and introduces new health features supported by the upcoming Galaxy Watch. Talking about design, the update introduces a streamlined layout with five categories: Sleep, Activity, Nutrition, Mindfulness, and Vitals. Users can now access their daily wellness tips and AI-powered Energy Score on the Home screen to easily track various aspects of their health. Regarding new features, Samsung has announced Vitals, Heart Health Score, Daily Cardio Load, and Fitness Index, which will be first...

title: iPhone 18 Pro Max's aluminum frame leaks in three colors
description: Today three colors for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro Max have allegedly been leaked. What you can see below are purported to be the aluminum body frames for the device, in "Dark Cherry", "Cloud Blue", and black. It's unclear if these will be Apple's marketing terms for the hues, but the company has long been rumored to introduce a "dark cherry" / "red wine" sort of colorway that will be the hero one for the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max, and this leak definitely gives us a better look at it. iPhone 18 Pro Max frame in Dark Cherry, Cloud Blue, and black The "Cloud Blue" is...

title: RedMagic 11S Pro review
